title: Non-Destructive View Exploration and Edit Mode for Object Views
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to explore saved views by toggling objects on and off without permanently changing the view, so that I can investigate different object configurations without accidentally overwriting a saved view.
description:
Saved views (formerly ERD views) allow users to toggle objects on and off. Previously, any change was immediately persisted to the view. This change introduces a non-destructive exploration mode and an explicit edit workflow.

Changes:
1. **Non-destructive exploration**: When viewing a saved view, toggling objects on/off does not persist changes. The user must enter edit mode and save to commit changes.
2. **Edit mode guidance**: When the user makes their first change to a saved view, a contextual message appears: 'You have unsaved changes. Click here to edit.' Clicking the link enters edit mode (same as the edit button). The message resets when switching views or saving.
3. **Rename ERD to Object Views**: The label 'ERD' is changed to 'Object Views' across the UI to prepare for reuse on the Object Dictionary page.
acceptance_criteria: Toggling objects in a saved view does not persist changes unless the user enters edit mode and saves
The 'All' view allows exploration without committing changes, consistent with saved views
A contextual message appears on first change: 'You have unsaved changes. Click here to edit.'
Clicking the contextual link enters edit mode
The message disappears when switching views or after saving
Label 'ERD' is renamed to 'Object Views' in the UI
ux_changes: Introduces non-destructive exploration mode for saved views. Adds an inline notification when unsaved changes are detected with a link to enter edit mode. Renames 'ERD' to 'Object Views' throughout the UI.
middleware_changes: View toggle changes are no longer auto-saved. Server-side save only fires when the user explicitly enters edit mode and commits.
implementation_notes: The rename from ERD to Object Views is preparation for reusing the same view functionality on the Object Dictionary page. The edit-mode gate prevents accidental overwrites when users are just exploring.
comment_summary: Developer implemented and deployed to staging for review. No significant design disagreements.
